signals.reasoning: BTC is stabilizing after the flush, but the active veto keeps fresh directional exposure unattractive. There is no strong enough edge here to override the event brake.
ETH still looks like a post-flush stabilization move rather than a clean breakout or fresh breakdown. Under veto, neutral is the disciplined call.
SOL is behaving more like a liquidation reset than a fresh continuation setup in either direction. That keeps it off the actionable list while veto is live.
CHILLGUY is already very extended and reads more like a short-covering rally than a clean durable trend turn. Too hot to chase and too strong to short cleanly.
DOOD has real squeeze fuel on paper, but the move is already underway and execution quality is still poor. Under veto, that is not enough to justify a fresh long.
0G still has the strongest squeeze profile on the board, but it is already extended and now sits under a live event brake. Best raw long is not the same as best actual trade here.
MON looks more like a short-covering bounce than a fresh clean short or a trustworthy long. The setup is too mixed to force under veto.
ZEREBRO is neutralizing after prior damage, but there is no strong new continuation in either direction. It does not clear the bar for a fresh signal.
FOGO is bouncing inside a damaged range without a clean structural edge. This is exactly the kind of middling board name we should leave alone.
STABLE still has real squeeze fuel, but it is already very extended and the live veto matters more than the raw funding signal. Better to keep it watch-only than chase it.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already too mature and unstable to trust as a clean fresh short. Weak structure alone is not enough.
